name: Machado de Assis
bio: Joaquim Maria Machado de Assis, often known by his surnames as Machado de Assis, Machado, or Bruxo do Cosme Velho, was a pioneer Brazilian novelist, poet, playwright and short story writer, widely regarded as the greatest writer of Brazilian literature. In 1897, he founded and became the first President of the Brazilian Academy of Letters. He was multilingual, having taught himself French, English, German and Greek later in life.

awards.name: Knight of the Imperial Order of the Rose
Officer of the Imperial Order of the Rose
Livro dos Heróis e Heroínas da Pátria
